endpoints.purpose: Read autoReplySettings + optInOutSettings fields from the projects document
Toggle autoReplySettings.masterEnabled on the project
Upsert autoReplySettings.welcomeMessage (enabled + message text)

proposedCrate.notes: All writes are $set patches on the projects collection — no new collection needed. Must invalidate the Meta webhook project cache (same pattern as the Next.js actions: call invalidateProjectCache after write). uxGaps: Enable/disable toggle per rule type (welcomeMessage, inactiveHours, general, aiAssistant) is handled client-side via hidden form field — toggling the switch does not immediately persist; user must still click Save. Consider an optimistic PATCH on toggle change.

General keyword rules (autoReplySettings.general.replies[]) are an inline array on the project document — at scale (many rules) this is a design smell; the advanced /auto-reply-rules page uses a proper wa_auto_reply_rules collection. Consider unifying.
AI assistant section has no preview/test-reply affordance — user cannot validate that the configured context produces expected outputs.

proposedCrate.name: wachat-contact-merge

endpoints.purpose: True contact merge: union all fields (name, phone, waId, custom variables, tagIds) from secondary into primary choosing non-null winner per field; reassign all wa_conversations and wa_messages from secondary contactId to primary; delete the secondary contact document. Returns the updated primary contact.

proposedCrate.notes: The current page only unions tagIds and leaves the secondary contact alive — a shallow workaround. A proper merge must: (1) field-level conflict resolution (primary wins by default, or user picks per-field); (2) re-point all conversation/message foreign keys from secondary._id to primary._id; (3) delete the secondary contact. The existing update_contact_tags handler in wachat-contacts is reused for the tag-union step; the new crate owns the orchestration transaction. The page's preview panel also only shows tagIds count — it should show all contact fields side-by-side for field-conflict resolution before the user confirms.

uxGaps: Merge preview panel only shows name, phone, and tag count — missing custom variables, assigned agent, status, and opt-out state for proper field-conflict resolution
No field-level conflict picker: user cannot choose which value wins per field (currently primary always wins implicitly)
Secondary contact is never deleted after merge — duplicate remains in the list
Conversation history is not redirected to the primary contact after merge
No pagination on the contacts list (only page 1 is loaded), making it hard to surface duplicates in large datasets
Search triggers on Enter/button click only — no debounced live search

Internal URL shortening proxy — accepts a long URL, stores a short_code + original_url in wa_short_links, returns the internal short URL (eliminates TinyURL dependency and keeps URLs on-domain)
